cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Teradata Autonomous Knowledge Platform Reaches General Availability Across Cloud and On Premises
stories.plain: Teradata's Autonomous Knowledge Platform is now GA across cloud, on-premises, and hybrid setups. It lets enterprises run agentic AI directly where their data already lives, without giving up cost control or explainability. This matters because most agentic AI deployments force a trade-off between capability and governance — Teradata is betting it can close that gap at enterprise scale.

stories.head: Creatio Takes Aim at the Enterprise Frankenstack
stories.plain: Creatio's updated platform combines CRM, AI agents, and workflow automation into one environment, directly challenging the patchwork of disconnected point solutions many enterprises rely on. This is significant because consolidating the stack changes how marketing AI is both bought and governed — fewer vendors means tighter loops between data, decisions, and execution.

stories.head: GetWhys Grounds All AI-Generated GTM Content in Real Buyer Intelligence
stories.plain: GetWhys plugs real buyer research into AI content pipelines so that go-to-market copy is anchored in what actual buyers say, not just what the model predicts. As teams ship more AI-generated messaging than ever, output quality governance becomes the key bottleneck. This tool addresses who controls what the AI says and why it says it.

stories.head: Bridgenext Content Studio Launches GEO Measurement Layer to Broadcast & PR Services
stories.plain: Bridgenext is adding a before-and-after GEO measurement layer to satellite media tours, showing clients how PR activity shifts what AI tools say about their brand. This is one of the first practical measurement tools for Generative Engine Optimization, making it relevant for anyone trying to prove ROI on AI-search visibility.

stories.head: Why AI-Ready Data Infrastructure Is So Hard to Build
stories.plain: A practical explainer on why most companies stall when trying to make their data AI-ready — and why tackling one high-value problem first beats boiling the ocean. For anyone buying or building marketing AI, this frames the real obstacle: it's not the model, it's the data foundation underneath it.

stories.head: Creatio takes aim at the enterprise Frankenstack with combined CRM, AI agents, and workflows
stories.plain: Creatio is pitching a single platform to replace the patchwork of disconnected CRM, automation, and analytics tools most enterprises run today. That matters because agentic AI only works well when it can see and act across a unified environment — fragmented stacks are the main reason AI projects stall before they deliver value.

stories.head: Teradata Autonomous Knowledge Platform reaches general availability for cloud, on-prem, and hybrid
stories.plain: Teradata is now shipping a full enterprise stack designed to run agentic AI where your data already lives, without forcing a cloud migration. For marketing teams, this changes the build-vs-buy calculus: organizations with on-prem data warehouses can now deploy autonomous decisioning without sacrificing governance or cost control.

stories.head: Marketing's AI challenge goes beyond adoption — research flags organizational readiness gaps
stories.plain: A new study finds that while marketers appreciate AI's efficiency gains, most worry their organizations aren't structurally ready to use it well. Adoption rates are high but readiness is low — a gap that directly affects how marketing AI budgets get approved and how projects get governed going forward.

stories.head: HubSpot CMO Kipp Bodnar on brand discovery and Answer Engine Optimization in the AI search era
stories.plain: HubSpot's CMO explains how brands need to rethink discoverability now that AI assistants — not search rankings — increasingly decide what customers find. He ties off-site citations and unified customer data directly to visibility in AI-generated answers, making this a practical GEO briefing from a major platform vendor.

stories.head: Why AI-ready data infrastructure is so hard to build — and how to start anyway
stories.plain: Many teams try to overhaul all their data before deploying AI, and most fail. This piece argues for starting with one high-value problem rather than a full-stack rebuild. It's a directly actionable framing for anyone deciding how to sequence AI investments or justify data infrastructure spending to leadership.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Adoption Is Universal — Differentiation Is Not
body: With AI adoption now touching 90% of marketing organisations, the competitive edge has shifted from 'do you use AI?' to 'what makes your AI output feel unmistakably yours?' Brands running the same foundation models on the same prompts risk producing indistinguishable content at scale — a commoditisation trap that no tool upgrade can fix.

The strategic answer is proprietary data, tone-of-voice constraints, and human editorial judgment layered on top of AI generation — not instead of it. HubSpot's CMO frames this as a brand-discovery problem: if AI search surfaces your content, it must carry enough identity signal to create preference, not just awareness.
ev: Agile Brand · Same tools, different brands
Agile Brand · HubSpot CMO on AI search discovery

themes.head: Data Infrastructure Is the Real AI Bottleneck
body: Every ambitious AI use-case in marketing — personalisation, predictive scoring, autonomous agents — breaks down at the same point: messy, siloed, or ungoverned data. MarTech's deep-dive confirms that building AI-ready infrastructure is less a technology problem than an organisational and governance one, often requiring months of remediation before a single model can be trusted.

Vendors are racing to close the gap: Teradata's Autonomous Knowledge Platform just hit general availability, Remesh Connect bridges cross-platform research data, and Creatio is explicitly targeting the 'Frankenstack' of incompatible point solutions. The message for decision-makers is that buying more AI tools before fixing data foundations is burning budget on a leaky pipeline.

themes.head: The Rise of Agentic & Autonomous AI in Workflows
body: AI is graduating from a content-generation assistant to an autonomous workflow actor. Banco do Brasil has embedded agentic AI into core banking workflows; Hermes Agent Desktop offers a practitioner-level entry point; and the CMS is being reframed as an AI operating system — orchestrating content, data, and decisions without constant human hand-holding.

This shift demands a new procurement mindset. Asking vendors five pointed questions before you buy — around data access, agent scope, override controls, and audit trails — is no longer optional hygiene; it is the difference between an agent that amplifies strategy and one that autonomously undermines it.
ev: Agile Brand · Banco do Brasil agentic AI

themes.head: AI Governance & Talent — The Soft Crisis Underneath the Hype
body: Adoption metrics look impressive on paper, but two quieter crises are building in parallel. First, no one has solved who trains the next generation of marketers when AI handles the entry-level analytical and copywriting work that used to teach craft. Junior roles are hollowing out faster than apprenticeship models can adapt.

Second, most organisations still lack a structured process for evaluating whether their AI investments are working. Running an AI audit before the next budgeting cycle — interrogating which tools deliver measurable lift versus which are shelfware — is fast becoming a baseline competency that separates mature AI operators from early adopters still chasing novelty.

themes.head: Measurement Is Being Rebuilt From Scratch
body: Open-source tools have made Marketing Mix Modelling accessible to teams that once couldn't afford it — but accessibility hasn't made it easier to run correctly. Practitioners are discovering that cheaper entry points still require serious statistical competence, clean longitudinal data, and clear causal assumptions, or the outputs mislead rather than inform budget decisions.

Simultaneously, entirely new measurement categories are emerging: Bridgenext's GEO Measurement Layer tracks brand presence in AI-generated answers across broadcast & PR channels, signalling that Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) is becoming a measurable discipline in its own right. Marketers now face a dual imperative — fixing classical attribution while instrumenting for an AI-mediated discovery world.
ev: MarTech · Open-source MMM harder than it looks
MarTech Series · GetWhys buyer intelligence for GTM

month.takeaway: The month's news collectively shows that the marketing industry has largely solved the question of AI adoption — the unsolved problem is now governance, data readiness, and human judgment about when and how AI acts autonomously.
